title: Travel & Accommodation
shortSummary: Plan a trip, check into accommodation, and handle travel situations in French.
culturalNote: France is the world's most visited country, with everything from Paris and the Riviera to mountains and vineyards. Accommodation ranges from hotels to gîtes (self-catering cottages) and chambres d'hôtes (bed and breakfasts). August is the big holiday month, when many French take their long summer break, so popular spots are busy. A polite Bonjour at every front desk is expected.

keyPhrases.english: I'd like to book a room
Do you have a room available?

keyPhrases.native: À quelle heure est le départ ?
keyPhrases.pronunciation: ah kel UHR eh luh day-PAR

keyPhrases.native: Y a-t-il un site à voir ?
keyPhrases.english: Is there a sight to see?
keyPhrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL un seet ah VWAHR

grammar.rule: à, en, and au for destinations
grammar.explanation: Use à for cities (à Paris), en for feminine countries (en France, en Italie), and au for masculine countries (au Canada, au Japon). For plural countries use aux: aux États-Unis.
grammar.commonMistake: Using à for countries: it is en France and au Japon, not à France.

grammar.rule: Passé composé for recounting a trip
grammar.explanation: To tell someone about a past trip, use the passé composé: J'ai visité Paris (I visited Paris). Movement verbs take être: Je suis allé en Italie.

examples.native: Je suis allé à la plage.
examples.en: I went to the beach. (être verb)

grammar.commonMistake: Using avoir with movement verbs: it is Je suis allé, not J'ai allé.

dialogue.setup: A guest arrives at a small hotel and speaks with the receptionist.

lines.speaker: Client
lines.english: Good evening, I have booked a room.

lines.speaker: Réception
lines.native: Bienvenue ! À quel nom, s'il vous plaît ?

lines.speaker: Client
lines.native: Au nom de Martin, pour deux nuits.
lines.english: Under the name Martin, for two nights.

lines.speaker: Réception
lines.native: Parfait. Le petit-déjeuner est de sept à dix heures.
lines.english: Perfect. Breakfast is from seven to ten.

lines.speaker: Client
lines.native: Très bien. À quelle heure est le départ ?
lines.english: Very good. What time is check-out?

lines.speaker: Réception
lines.native: À midi. Voici votre clé, chambre douze.
lines.english: At noon. Here is your key, room twelve.

lines.speaker: Client
lines.english: Thank you very much. Have a good evening!

quiz.question: How do you say 'I'd like to book a room'?
quiz.explanation: Je voudrais plus the infinitive réserver.

quiz.question: Nous allons _____ France. (to, feminine country)
quiz.explanation: Feminine countries take en: en France.

quiz.question: Which is correct for a masculine country (le Japon)?
quiz.explanation: Masculine countries take au: au Japon.

quiz.question: Which sentence is correct in the past?
quiz.explanation: aller takes être, and Italy (feminine) takes en.

quiz.question: Arrange: chambre / une / libre / avez-vous
quiz.explanation: Avez-vous (do you have) + une chambre libre (a free room).
